---
title: "State-wise Labour Market Comparison"
subtitle: "Deep Dive into Regional Labour Force Patterns"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
  docx:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
params:
  data_file: "plfs_2022_23_person"
  survey_year: "2022-23"
  focus_states: null  # Set to c("Maharashtra", "Tamil Nadu") to focus on specific states
  compare_national: true
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

source(here::here("R/01_config.R"))
source(here::here("R/03_read_microdata.R"))
source(here::here("R/04_survey_design.R"))
source(here::here("R/06_plfs_indicators.R"))
source(here::here("R/08_codebook_utils.R"))
source(here::here("R/09_export_tables.R"))
source(here::here("R/10_viz_themes.R"))

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

# Overview

This report provides a detailed state-wise comparison of labour market indicators from PLFS `r params$survey_year`.

```{r load-data}
#| label: load-data

persons <- load_from_parquet(params$data_file)
persons <- decode_all(persons, state_col = "State", sex_col = "Sex", sector_col = "Sector")
design <- create_plfs_design(persons)
```

---

# State Rankings

## Labour Force Participation Rate

```{r lfpr-ranking}
#| label: lfpr-ranking
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 12

by_state <- calc_indicators_by_state(design, add_names = TRUE)
setorder(by_state, -lfpr)

# National average for reference
national <- calc_all_indicators(design)
nat_lfpr <- national$lfpr

# Add comparison to national
by_state[, lfpr_diff := lfpr - nat_lfpr]
by_state[, above_national := lfpr_diff > 0]

# Plot
ggplot(by_state[!is.na(state_name)], 
       aes(x = reorder(state_name, lfpr), y = lfpr, fill = above_national)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nat_lfpr, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = nat_lfpr + 2, 
           label = sprintf("National: %.1f%%", nat_lfpr), 
           color = "red", size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", lfpr)), hjust = -0.2, size = 2.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#2E5B88", "FALSE" = "#E57200"), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(by_state$lfpr) * 1.15)) +
  labs(
    title = sprintf("Labour Force Participation Rate by State, PLFS %s", params$survey_year),
    subtitle = "Red dashed line = National average",
    x = NULL,
    y = "LFPR (%)"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
```

## Unemployment Rate

```{r ur-ranking}
#| label: ur-ranking
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 12

setorder(by_state, -ur)
nat_ur <- national$ur

by_state[, ur_diff := ur - nat_ur]
by_state[, ur_above_national := ur_diff > 0]

ggplot(by_state[!is.na(state_name)], 
       aes(x = reorder(state_name, ur), y = ur, fill = ur_above_national)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = nat_ur, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = nat_ur + 0.5, 
           label = sprintf("National: %.1f%%", nat_ur),
           color = "red", size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", ur)), hjust = -0.2, size = 2.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#E57200", "FALSE" = "#2E5B88"), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(by_state$ur) * 1.2)) +
  labs(
    title = sprintf("Unemployment Rate by State, PLFS %s", params$survey_year),
    subtitle = "Orange = Above national average (higher unemployment)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Unemployment Rate (%)"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
```

---

# Gender Gap Analysis

```{r gender-gap}
#| label: gender-gap

by_state_sex <- calc_all_indicators(design, by = c("State_name", "Sex"))

# Calculate gender gap
gender_gap <- dcast(by_state_sex, State_name ~ Sex, value.var = "lfpr")
gender_gap[, gender_gap := Male - Female]
setorder(gender_gap, -gender_gap)
```

```{r gender-gap-plot}
#| label: gender-gap-plot
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-cap: "Gender Gap in LFPR (Male - Female)"

ggplot(gender_gap[!is.na(State_name)], 
       aes(x = reorder(State_name, -gender_gap), y = gender_gap)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#8B0000", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", gender_gap)), hjust = -0.2, size = 2.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Gender Gap in Labour Force Participation",
    subtitle = sprintf("PLFS %s | Gap = Male LFPR - Female LFPR", params$survey_year),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Gender Gap (percentage points)"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))
```

```{r gender-gap-table}
#| label: gender-gap-table

# Top 10 largest gaps
kable(gender_gap[1:10, .(State_name, Male = round(Male, 1), Female = round(Female, 1), 
                          `Gap (pp)` = round(gender_gap, 1))],
      caption = "States with Largest Gender Gap in LFPR")
```

---

# Rural-Urban Divide

```{r rural-urban}
#| label: rural-urban

by_state_sector <- calc_all_indicators(design, by = c("State_name", "Sector"))

# Rural-Urban gap
ru_gap <- dcast(by_state_sector, State_name ~ Sector, value.var = "lfpr")
ru_gap[, ru_gap := Rural - Urban]
setorder(ru_gap, -ru_gap)
```

```{r ru-gap-plot}
#| label: ru-gap-plot
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-cap: "Rural-Urban Gap in LFPR"

ggplot(ru_gap[!is.na(State_name)], 
       aes(x = reorder(State_name, ru_gap), y = ru_gap, 
           fill = ru_gap > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#6B8E23", "FALSE" = "#2E5B88"),
                    labels = c("Urban > Rural", "Rural > Urban"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Rural-Urban Gap in LFPR by State",
    subtitle = sprintf("PLFS %s | Gap = Rural LFPR - Urban LFPR", params$survey_year),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Rural-Urban Gap (percentage points)"
  ) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

# Regional Patterns

```{r regional}
#| label: regional

# Load state codes with regions
states <- load_state_codes()

# Merge region info
by_state_region <- merge(by_state, states[, .(state_code, region)], 
                          by = "state_code", all.x = TRUE)

# Regional averages (unweighted)
regional <- by_state_region[!is.na(region), .(
  lfpr = mean(lfpr, na.rm = TRUE),
  wpr = mean(wpr, na.rm = TRUE),
  ur = mean(ur, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_states = .N
), by = region]

setorder(regional, -lfpr)
```

```{r regional-table}
#| label: regional-table

kable(regional[, .(Region = region, 
                    `LFPR (%)` = round(lfpr, 1),
                    `WPR (%)` = round(wpr, 1),
                    `UR (%)` = round(ur, 1),
                    `States` = n_states)],
      caption = "Regional Average Labour Force Indicators")
```

```{r regional-plot}
#| label: regional-plot
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(regional, aes(x = reorder(region, -lfpr), y = lfpr)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2E5B88", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", lfpr)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(regional$lfpr) * 1.15)) +
  labs(
    title = "Average LFPR by Region",
    subtitle = sprintf("PLFS %s | Simple average across states in region", params$survey_year),
    x = NULL,
    y = "LFPR (%)"
  ) +
  theme_publication()
```

---

# Summary Statistics Table

```{r summary-table}
#| label: summary-table

summary_table <- by_state[!is.na(state_name), .(
  State = state_name,
  `LFPR (%)` = round(lfpr, 1),
  `WPR (%)` = round(wpr, 1),
  `UR (%)` = round(ur, 1),
  `Sample (N)` = format(n, big.mark = ",")
)]

setorder(summary_table, State)

kable(summary_table, 
      caption = sprintf("Labour Force Indicators by State, PLFS %s", params$survey_year),
      align = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r"))
```

---

# Export

```{r export}
#| label: export
#| eval: false

# Export state comparison table
export_indicator_table(
  by_state[!is.na(state_name)],
  sprintf("state_comparison_%s", gsub("-", "_", params$survey_year)),
  title = sprintf("State-wise Labour Force Indicators, PLFS %s", params$survey_year)
)

# Export gender gap table
export_summary_table(
  gender_gap[!is.na(State_name)],
  sprintf("gender_gap_%s", gsub("-", "_", params$survey_year)),
  title = "Gender Gap in LFPR by State"
)
```

---

*Report generated on `r Sys.Date()`*
